title: 5K Training Calculator
explanation: Estimate calorie burn and plan weekly training volume for a first 5k based on bodyweight, target time, and weeks of preparation.

cells:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

bodyweight_kg = bodyweight_kg
goal_time_min = goal_time_min
weeks = weeks
pace_min_per_km = goal_time_min / 5
calories_per_run = bodyweight_kg * 5 * 0.5  # kcal
weekly_runs = 3
total_calories = calories_per_run * weekly_runs * weeks
print(f"Estimated total calories over {weeks} weeks: {total_calories:.1f}")

cum_cal = np.arange(1, weeks+1) * calories_per_run * weekly_runs
plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
plt.plot(cum_cal, marker='o')
plt.title('Cumulative Calories Over Training Weeks')
plt.xlabel('Week')
plt.ylabel('Calories (kcal)')
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Simple bar chart of weekly mileage (km) assuming 3 runs per week at half the 5k distance
weekly_km = 2.5 * 3  # half 5k per run
weeks_array = np.arange(1, weeks+1)
plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
plt.bar(weeks_array, weekly_km * weeks_array, color='skyblue')
plt.title('Weekly Mileage (km)')
plt.xlabel('Week')
plt.ylabel('Kilometers')
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.show()

title: Running Schedule Planner
explanation: It lets you adjust training parameters to see how many kilometers you’ll run each week.

title: Weekly Running Volume Calculator
explanation: It shows how many miles you’ll run each week based on your chosen schedule, helping you build a consistent running habit.

cells:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def weekly_volume(runs, miles):
    return runs * miles

total = weekly_volume(runs_per_week, distance_miles)
print(f"Total weekly mileage: {total} miles")
plt.bar(['Mon','Tue','Wed','Thu','Fri','Sat','Sun'], [distance_miles]*runs_per_week + [0]*(7-runs_per_week))
plt.title('Weekly Running Schedule')
plt.ylabel('Miles')
plt.show()
